digest.greeting: Monday, June 15, Jean. Outlook shows 6 working-day calendar blocks, anchored by the 9:30 AM TRACE & Staff Aug touchpoint and the 2:00 PM EPMO Portfolio Review. There is 1 carried-over item and 2 active reminders; the strongest priorities are confirming the Staff Aug at-risk path before the Yuri touchpoint, closing the HR survey-results follow-up, replying on the Venn issues thread, and using the EPMO Portfolio Review to assign intake next steps. Broad Teams recent-thread discovery still returned a Microsoft Graph 403, and targeted Teams searches did not return new Staff Aug, Venn, or HR-survey activity after Friday, so Teams context is limited this morning.

items.label: Plan Lead Up Module 2 pre-work
items.detail: Carried over from Friday and still not marked complete in GitHub state. Rippling enrolled Jean in Lead Up Module 2 Pre Work due June 25 at 8:06 AM; use one of today's short open windows to decide where this fits before the week fills in.

items.label: Follow up with HR on survey results
items.detail: One-shot dashboard reminder added June 12: "Hacer follow up con HR para resultados de la survey." This is a clean early follow-up before the schedule compresses around the EPMO Portfolio Review.

items.label: Reply on the Venn issues thread for Eugenia and Danny
items.detail: One-shot dashboard reminder added June 12. The prior digest noted Nadia's additional Venn pain points around slowness, access, app compatibility, Teams issues, and failed authentication; reply or assign the next owner before the thread goes stale.

sections.title: Today's Schedule
sections.note: Times shown in Jean's Outlook working timezone. The day has good morning focus windows, then becomes meeting-heavy from noon through 3:30 PM.

items.label: Approve Timecards & Prepare for the Week
items.detail: One-hour prep block. Best use is timecards, quick review of the unread Asana Staff Aug portfolio signal, and selecting the few Monday follow-ups that need to move before noon.

items.label: TRACE & Staff Aug touchpoint
items.detail: Short Yuri touchpoint. Staff Aug is the key prep signal because the unread Asana portfolio report marks the project at risk and very high impact, with tracker work running until June 26 and PRD stakeholder review continuing this week.

items.label: 1:1 Shelley & Jean
items.detail: Agenda says the purpose is to understand department needs and how Jean can support. Good place to surface any project-management support, portfolio-routing friction, or quality/process needs before the afternoon review.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Protected 90-minute block before EPMO Portfolio Review. Best use is the HR survey-results reminder, Venn reply, and any Staff Aug notes from the Yuri touchpoint.

items.label: EPMO Portfolio Review
items.detail: Sara's review focuses on new project requests, EPMO classification, missing info, cross-org impacts, intake ownership, PM/PE assignment, and rejection communication. Bring only the items that need portfolio-level routing.

items.label: Touchpoint w/Jean
items.detail: Licelot touchpoint on process and quality-assurance requirements inside the EPMO. Useful follow-through from the portfolio review if QA or process guidance needs a named next step.

items.label: 9:00-9:30, 9:45-11:00, 11:30-12:00, 1:30-2:00, and after 3:30
items.detail: The 9:45-11:00 window is the best uninterrupted block for Staff Aug follow-through; the 12:00 protected space is better for reminders and portfolio-review prep.

items.label: Confirm Staff Aug at-risk path with Yuri
items.detail: Action type: decide/follow up. The unread Asana PMO portfolio report marks Staff Augmentation at risk and very high impact: Data Team tracker improvements run until June 26, geographic KPI work is in progress, cost KPIs are still under analysis, and stakeholder PRD review continues this week. Use the 9:30 AM TRACE & Staff Aug touchpoint to confirm the next decision, owner, and risk language.

items.label: Prepare EPMO Portfolio Review routing decisions
items.detail: Action type: prepare/decide. The 2:00 PM review is explicitly for classifying new project requests, identifying missing info, assigning intake calls, naming PM/PE ownership, and deciding rejection communication. Bring any Staff Aug, CoE, Phase 2, or process/QA items that need portfolio-level routing rather than letting them remain general discussion.

items.label: Use Shelley and Licelot touchpoints to clarify support needs
items.detail: Action type: prepare/follow up. Shelley's 1:1 is framed around department needs and how Jean can support; Licelot's touchpoint is framed around EPMO processes and quality assurance. Capture any blocker, owner, or process decision that should feed back into the portfolio review or tomorrow's PM Workshop.

items.label: Unread Asana report flags Staff Aug at risk
items.detail: The weekly PMO Projects Portfolio report shows Staff Augmentation owned by Yuri as at risk and very high impact. It calls out Data Team tracker work through June 26, KPI analysis, and PRD stakeholder review continuing this week; this is the strongest email signal tied to today's 9:30 AM touchpoint.

items.label: 1:1 Conor B. & Jean G.
items.detail: Agenda includes department needs, support, and project-management principles.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Protected midday space before the afternoon project-management block.

items.label: PM Workshop Pt.2
items.detail: Next topic is controlling projects through stakeholders, with Mar, Yuri, and Jhara invited.

items.label: 1:1 Yuri & Jean, then 1:1 Jhara & Jean
items.detail: Yuri at 3:00 PM and Jhara at 3:30 PM create a tight end-of-day portfolio and roadblock review block.

items.label: Scheduled reminder: COE playbook
items.detail: Dashboard state has a reminder scheduled for June 16: Seguimiento a David para la creacion del COE playbook.

reminders.text: Seguimiento a David para la creación del COE playbook

reminderHistory.text: Seguimiento a stakeholders de Co-counsel network project. Go/no-go?
Hacer follow up con HR para resultados de la survey
Responder al correo de Eugenia para Danny sobre problemas con Venn
